risks.title: Default npm test can run under Node 16 and create misleading failures
acceptance_gates: npm test must use the Node18 wrapper or fail early with a clear Node version message
node tools/run-with-node18.mjs --test test/*.test.js

resolution.summary: Default npm test and test:coverage now run through tools/run-with-node18.mjs, with a regression test that locks wrapper usage.

evidence.summary: Current default shell reported Node v16.17.1 while Node18 wrapper path passed the full suite in the prior repair run.
Targeted script and runner tests passed after routing npm test/test:coverage through the Node18 wrapper.
Default npm test entrypoint executed through tools/run-with-node18.mjs and passed the full suite.
Default npm test:coverage entrypoint executed through tools/run-with-node18.mjs and passed coverage thresholds.
DeepSeek read-only review passed with no blocking findings.
DeepSeek final closeout review passed with no blocking findings; its non-blocking regex hardening recommendation was applied.

non_blocking_findings: The known-risk-closeout-runner test changed from asserting git-worktree path diffs to asserting ledger/artifact state invariants. This is a stronger, concurrency-safe assertion pattern and acceptable, but the diff summary should be documented in the risk closeout notes for full traceability.
The original test:coverage regex was start-anchored only. It still verified wrapper usage, but a trailing anchor would better detect suffix tampering. This was hardened before final commit.

resolution.summary: Extracted agent key and agent health API routes from the mixed Workbench server entrypoint into tools/workbench-agent-key-routes.mjs, preserved existing API coverage, added fail-closed coverage for missing SQLite state, and updated large-file governance metadata.

evidence.summary: tools/workbench-server.mjs is registered as planned_refactor and remains a large mixed HTTP/service boundary.
First bounded route handler extraction completed for agent key CRUD, agent health checks, and agent role updates.
Workbench server API regression suite passed after extraction.

DeepSeek reviewed the expanded gate repair and found no gate relaxation or blocking closeout issue.
Full npm test suite passed after the route extraction and route-contract gate repair.
Coverage gate passed after the route extraction and route-contract gate repair; reported all-files line coverage 91.85% and function coverage 96.92%.
Full closeout gate passed after installing the worktree npm dependencies required by the public browser route check.

non_blocking_findings: Shard-level inconclusive notes were caused by split file visibility; the synthesis verdict was PASS after reviewing both server/module and test/manifest shards.
The remaining projectionInputWithProjectStatus stateStore.summarizeAgentRegistry coupling was pre-existing and outside this bounded extraction.
resolution.fixed_by_commit used a short 7-character hash in the reviewed closeout record; the ledger was updated afterward to use the full 40-character commit hash.
The sharded reviewer artifact explains that shard-level inconclusive notes were resolved by synthesis PASS, which is acceptable for this bounded closeout.
release.required is recorded as false for an internal route-boundary refactor; future schema tests could cover release metadata.
The backend_extraction_empty error message still names the historical server file instead of all backend route source files; this is cosmetic and does not affect gate accuracy.
The array-source extractor path is covered by the real multi-source API contract test shape rather than a separate synthetic unit test.

release.reason: No user-visible runtime route changed; this is an internal server route-boundary refactor with preserved API behavior.

risks.title: Frontend API client drops structured backend diagnostics on non-2xx responses
acceptance_gates: Non-2xx JSON error bodies preserve error, phase, issues, and projection when present
node tools/run-with-node18.mjs --test test/workbench-shell.test.js
node ../../tools/run-with-node18.mjs node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build

resolution.summary: Added WorkbenchApiError so fetchWorkbenchJson preserves non-2xx JSON response diagnostics as structured error, phase, issues, and projection fields, with behavior tests for full JSON, partial JSON, and non-JSON error bodies. Final closeout also removed a divergent --test-force-exit unit-test mode from check:closeout after it hung post-summary in the isolated worktree, aligning closeout unit tests with the standard npm test path and adding a 15-minute child-process timeout so future hangs fail explicitly.

evidence.summary: fetchWorkbenchJson currently throws only `workbench api <path> failed: <status>` for non-2xx responses.
Workbench API client now throws WorkbenchApiError with structured diagnostics on non-2xx JSON responses.
API client behavior tests and existing workbench shell tests passed after preserving structured diagnostics.
Next.js workbench production build passed after the API error type change.
DeepSeek implementation and delta reviews passed with no blocking findings; the non-blocking partial-field test recommendation was applied.
Known-risk ledger structural gate passed after marking the API diagnostics risk fixed; remaining open risks are outside this scoped closeout.

DeepSeek closeout review passed with no blocking findings; it confirmed fixed status, full commit hash, evidence, release decision, and known-risk counts. Its evidence-thinness note is addressed by final full-suite and closeout gates before merge.
Full repository Node test suite passed after the API diagnostics change and closeout test-runner alignment.
Coverage gate passed with 948/948 tests, all-files line coverage 91.91% and function coverage 96.93%.
Package script regression test passed after removing the divergent --test-force-exit mode from check:closeout unit tests and adding timeout protection.
First check:closeout attempt failed because the isolated worktree root lacked the playwright package; npm ci installed root dependencies with Node16 engine warnings only.
A subsequent check:closeout attempt printed 948/948 unit tests passed but hung in the --test-force-exit child process; this exposed a closeout gate exit-mode risk during final verification.

Full closeout gate passed after aligning check:closeout unit tests with the standard non-forced test-runner mode and adding child-process timeout protection; unit tests reported 949/949 and all closeout sub-gates passed.
DeepSeek final gate-patch and ledger-packet reviews passed with no blocking findings after the closeout scope expansion and timeout protection.
Final known-risk ledger structural gate passed after all evidence and reviewer artifact updates.

Required known-risk closeout mode failed only for the three remaining open risks outside this scoped closeout: code-review coverage denominator, Playwright CLI artifact cleanliness, and useProjection request ordering.

non_blocking_findings: When init.signal is provided by the caller, the internal 30s AbortController timeout is bypassed because fetch listens to the caller signal; this pre-existing design behavior is outside this structured diagnostics risk.
DeepSeek suggested adding a partial-field JSON body test. The test was added and the delta review passed; the delta review noted message format is already covered by the full JSON-body test.

The final closeout scope expansion added tools/check-closeout.mjs and test/package-scripts.test.js after check:closeout exposed a unit-test child-process exit hang; DeepSeek reviewed the final gate patch and found no blocking issue.
The final ledger packet review passed; test counts moved from 948 to 949 because the closeout exit-mode regression test was added.
The closeout artifact was untracked at review time and must be committed with the ledger update before merge.

release.reason: No user-visible runtime route changed; this is a frontend API client error-object improvement with preserved build and shell behavior.

risks.title: Durable code-review coverage artifact does not cover all Workbench dashboard entrypoints

npm run check:code-review-coverage -- docs/examples/code-review-coverage-current.json

resolution.summary: Expanded the durable code-review coverage artifact from 20 to 43 first-party files so it includes all current Workbench app routes, shell, layout/provider/theme/error/loading/not-found files, dashboard subroutes, API clients, hooks, and task-flow layer. Added a regression test that enumerates the current apps/workbench/app and apps/workbench/lib source tree and fails when any Workbench source file is missing from the artifact denominator.

evidence.summary: Prior repair expanded the coverage artifact to 20 files but did not include every Workbench app entrypoint and shared hook/API layer.
code-review-coverage-current now covers 43 first-party files across Workbench app/lib, server, workflow, and supporting tests, with 6 passing shards and no missing shard count.
Coverage dispatch tests passed, including the new current Workbench app/lib source tree denominator guard.
Code-review coverage gate passed with first_party_file_count 43, shard_count 6, dispatch_shard_count 0, and no issues.
Workbench API client, shell, and coverage dispatch tests passed together after expanding the denominator.

Full repository test suite passed under the Node wrapper after the denominator expansion.
Coverage gate passed with 950/950 tests, all-files line coverage 91.78%, and all-files function coverage 96.93%.
Repository closeout gate passed after installing worktree-local root dependencies for the Playwright-backed public browser route check.
Known-risk ledger gate passed with risk_count 7, open_count 2, terminal_count 5.
Required-closed mode failed only for the two remaining scoped-out open risks: risk-20260601-playwright-cli-artifact-cleanliness and risk-20260601-use-projection-request-order.
DeepSeek precommit closeout review passed with no blocking findings after inspecting the ledger entry, reviewer artifact, and denominator drift regression test.

non_blocking_findings: The dynamic Workbench source guard initially covered only css/ts/tsx and lacked build/dependency directory exclusions; final patch covers js/jsx/mjs/cjs/ts/tsx/mts/cts/css and skips .next, node_modules, tmp, dist, and build.
The broader src/tools/test denominator has no equivalent dynamic drift guard; this remains outside the scoped Workbench dashboard entrypoint risk.

release.reason: No user-visible runtime behavior changed; this is a durable governance coverage artifact and regression-test update.

risks.title: Playwright CLI verification can leave untracked .playwright-cli artifacts in canonical checkout
acceptance_gates: Canonical checkout remains clean after Playwright CLI verification or .playwright-cli is ignored by policy
git status --short

resolution.summary: Ignored the local .playwright-cli/ directory, documented Playwright CLI and browser verification diagnostics as local git-ignored artifacts, and added a regression test that creates a .playwright-cli console log and asserts it does not appear in git status.

evidence.summary: A prior browser verification created `.playwright-cli/console-2026-06-01T06-25-17-467Z.log` in canonical checkout.
.gitignore now ignores `.playwright-cli/`, and PROJECT_RULES documents Playwright CLI/browser verification console logs as local temporary diagnostics that must not dirty canonical checkout.
Regression test passed; it checks the ignore rule, project policy text, `git check-ignore`, and a generated `.playwright-cli/console-test.log` with clean git status for that path.
Generated a sample `.playwright-cli/console-check.log`; `git check-ignore` returned the sample path and `git status --short -- .playwright-cli` produced no output.
Related regression tests passed across Playwright artifact cleanliness, known-risk ledger, and frontend acceptance hard-gate wiring.
DeepSeek compact implementation review passed with no blocking or non-blocking findings after a broader read-only review timed out inconclusively.
Known-risk ledger structural gate passed before marking the risk terminal.
Known-risk required-closed mode failed only for the remaining scoped-out useProjection request ordering risk.
Full repository test suite passed under the Node wrapper after adding the Playwright CLI artifact cleanliness guard.
Coverage gate passed with 952/952 tests, all-files line coverage 91.91%, and all-files function coverage 96.93%.
Repository closeout gate passed after installing worktree-local root dependencies for the Playwright-backed public browser route check.
DeepSeek final closeout review passed with no blocking or non-blocking findings after inspecting the compressed evidence packet.

release.reason: No user-visible runtime behavior changed; this is a local artifact ignore policy, documentation, and regression-test update.

risks.title: useProjection can allow older overlapping refresh responses to overwrite newer data
acceptance_gates: Polling/manual refresh uses AbortController or request sequence guard to prevent stale response overwrite
node tools/run-with-node18.mjs --test test/workbench-shell.test.js
node ../../tools/run-with-node18.mjs node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build

resolution.summary: Added AbortController plus request-sequence guards to useProjection and useProjectionHistory, threaded RequestInit through projection API fetchers for AbortSignal support, and added regression assertions for the guarded projection/history request ordering behavior. The Workbench shell large-file manifest was updated after the new regression increased its line count.

evidence.summary: useProjection currently starts fetches without aborting or sequencing overlapping refreshes.

useProjection and useProjectionHistory now abort stale in-flight requests, assign monotonically increasing request ids, and only update state or clear the active controller when the completing request is still current.
Projection API fetchers now accept RequestInit so hook callers can pass AbortSignal without changing the shared fetchWorkbenchJson behavior.
Workbench shell regression test passed, including static assertions for projection/history AbortController usage, request-id guards, RequestInit API signatures, and guarded null cleanup.
Related API client and code-review coverage dispatch tests passed after threading RequestInit through the projection API layer.
Next.js Workbench production build passed after installing worktree-local app dependencies.
Governance enrollment test passed after updating the Workbench shell large-file manifest entry to the new 845-line count and removing the duplicate stale entry.
Full repository test suite passed under the Node18 wrapper after the request-ordering fix and manifest correction.
Coverage gate passed with 953/953 tests, all-files line coverage 91.88%, and all-files function coverage 96.93%.
Code-review coverage artifact gate passed with 43 first-party files covered by six shards and no supplemental dispatch packages.

Repository closeout gate passed after installing root worktree dependencies needed for the Playwright-backed public browser route check; public browser route rendered with HTTP 200 and no console errors. Canonical publish verification is still required after merge because this risk changes Workbench client runtime code.

DeepSeek implementation review passed with no blocking findings; its two non-blocking findings were addressed by strengthening the regression assertion and applying the same stale-response guard to useProjectionHistory.
DeepSeek compact delta review passed with no blocking or non-blocking findings after confirming both previous non-blocking items were resolved.
Canonical Workbench LaunchAgent was restarted from the main checkout after fast-forward merge, publishing the updated Workbench client runtime.

Public mounted Workbench route probe passed against the expected /projects/ai-control-platform/projects/ route with HTTP 200, mounted workbench rendering, mounted API verification, no auth redirect, and non-loopback public route evidence.
Public browser route check passed after canonical publish: HTTP 200, Workbench rendered, root Next script count 0, console error count 0, and failed request count 0.
Live-route acceptance gate passed using the new post-merge public evidence artifact.

DeepSeek final post-merge closeout review passed with no blocking findings after evaluating canonical publish, public browser route verification, durable live-route evidence, known-risk required mode, and the remaining evidence commit scope.

release.reason: Workbench client runtime behavior changed, so the canonical mainline Workbench LaunchAgent was restarted and the public mounted route plus browser route were verified after merge.
